name: fetch-mock
description: Library for mocking calls to fetch in Node.js and browsers, supporting complex matchers, named routes and global/sandbox modes.

seo.title: fetch-mock — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: fetch-mock: Library for mocking calls to fetch in Node.js and browsers, supporting complex matchers, named routes and global/sandbox modes.

when_to_use: Mock fetch calls in Node.js and browser test suites
Named route matchers for complex URL pattern stubs
Sandbox mode for test isolation without globals
Simulate network errors and timeout scenarios

pros: Mocks fetch in Node and browsers with one API
Rich matchers, named routes and sandbox mode
Works with Jest, Vitest, Mocha, Karma
cons: Another layer versus MSW service worker approach
Global patching can leak between tests
Docs structure is dense for newcomers
Less realistic than intercepting at network level
review.verdict: Pragmatic fetch mocker that is fine until you outgrow it and want MSW-level fidelity.
review.best_for: Node-side units that only need to stub fetch calls quickly
review.not_for: Frontend integration tests better served by Mock Service Worker

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free/open-source — no commercial pricing, no HWM.
